en: Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses' father-in-law, heard of all that God had done for Moses and for Israel his people, how the LORD had brought Israel out of Egypt.
zh: 摩西的岳父，米甸祭司葉忒羅，聽見了 神為摩西和他自己的人民以色列所行的一切，就是耶和華怎樣把以色列人從埃及領出來的事，

en: Now Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, had taken Zipporah, Moses' wife, after he had sent her home,
zh: 他就帶著摩西的妻子西坡拉，摩西從前送了她回去，

en: along with her two sons. The name of the one was Gershom (for he said, "I have been a sojourner in a foreign land"),
zh: 又帶著西坡拉的兩個兒子，一個名叫革舜，因為摩西說：“我在異地作了客旅”；

en: and the name of the other, Eliezer (for he said, "The God of my father was my help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh").
zh: 一個名叫以利以謝，因為他說：“我父親的 神是我的幫助，救我脫離了法老的刀。”

en: Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, came with his sons and his wife to Moses in the wilderness where he was encamped at the mountain of God.
zh: 摩西的岳父葉忒羅，和摩西的妻子與兩個兒子，來到曠野摩西那裡，就是到 神的山，摩西安營的地方。

en: And when he sent word to Moses, "I, your father-in-law Jethro, am coming to you with your wife and her two sons with her,"
zh: 葉忒羅叫人告訴摩西說：“你的岳父葉忒羅來見你，你的妻子和兩個兒子也一同來了。”

en: Moses went out to meet his father-in-law and bowed down and kissed him. And they asked each other of their welfare and went into the tent.
zh: 摩西出來迎接他的岳父，向他下拜，與他親吻，彼此問候，然後一起進了帳棚。

en: Then Moses told his father-in-law all that the LORD had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel's sake, all the hardship that had come upon them in the way, and how the LORD had delivered them.
zh: 摩西把耶和華為以色列的緣故，向法老和埃及人所作的一切，以及路上遭遇的一切困難，並且耶和華怎樣拯救他們，都向他的岳父述說。

en: And Jethro rejoiced for all the good that the LORD had done to Israel, in that he had delivered them out of the hand of the Egyptians.
zh: 葉忒羅因為耶和華為以色列人所行的美事，就是拯救他們脫離埃及人的手，就非常喜樂。

en: Jethro said, "Blessed be the LORD, who has delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians and out of the hand of Pharaoh and has delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.
zh: 葉忒羅說：“耶和華是應當稱頌的，他拯救了你們脫離埃及人的手和法老的手；他也把這人民從埃及人的手下拯救出來。

en: Now I know that the LORD is greater than all gods, because in this affair they dealt arrogantly with the people."
zh: 現在我知道耶和華為至大，超乎萬神之上，因為這在埃及人以狂傲的態度對以色列人的事上已經證明了。”

en: And Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, brought a burnt offering and sacrifices to God; and Aaron came with all the elders of Israel to eat bread with Moses' father-in-law before God.
zh: 摩西的岳父葉忒羅把燔祭和幾樣祭品拿來獻給 神；亞倫和所有以色列的長老都來到 神面前，與摩西的岳父一同吃飯。

en: The next day Moses sat to judge the people, and the people stood around Moses from morning till evening.
zh: 次日，摩西坐著審判人民的案件；人民從早到晚都站在摩西的身旁。

en: When Moses' father-in-law saw all that he was doing for the people, he said, "What is this that you are doing for the people? Why do you sit alone, and all the people stand around you from morning till evening?"
zh: 摩西的岳父看見了摩西向人民所行的一切，就說：“你向人民作的是甚麼呢？你為甚麼獨自坐著審判，所有的人民卻從早到晚都站在你的身旁呢？”

en: And Moses said to his father-in-law, "Because the people come to me to inquire of God;
zh: 摩西回答他岳父：“因為人民到我這裡來求問 神。

en: when they have a dispute, they come to me and I decide between one person and another, and I make them know the statutes of God and his laws."
zh: 他們有事件的時候，就到我這裡來，我就在雙方之間施行審判，使他們認識 神的條例和律法。”

en: Moses' father-in-law said to him, "What you are doing is not good.
zh: 摩西的岳父對摩西說：“你這樣作不好。

en: You and the people with you will certainly wear yourselves out, for the thing is too heavy for you. You are not able to do it alone.
zh: 你必疲乏不堪；不但你自己，連與你在一起的人民也必疲乏不堪，因為這事太重，你不能獨自一人去作。

en: Now obey my voice; I will give you advice, and God be with you! You shall represent the people before God and bring their cases to God,
zh: 現在你要聽我的話，我要給你出個主意，願 神與你同在；你要代替人民到 神面前，把他們的事件帶到 神那裡。

en: and you shall warn them about the statutes and the laws, and make them know the way in which they must walk and what they must do.
zh: 又要把規例和律法教導他們，使他們知道他們當走的路，應作的事。

en: Moreover, look for able men from all the people, men who fear God, who are trustworthy and hate a bribe, and place such men over the people as chiefs of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens.
zh: 你要從人民中間選出有才能的人，就是敬畏 神、忠實可信、恨惡不義之財的人，設立他們作千夫長、百夫長、五十夫長、十夫長，去管理人民，

en: And let them judge the people at all times. Every great matter they shall bring to you, but any small matter they shall decide themselves. So it will be easier for you, and they will bear the burden with you.
zh: 使他們隨時可以審判人民的案件；所有大事件，他們要帶到你這裡來；所有小事件，他們可以自己審判。這樣，你就輕省些，他們也可以和你一同分擔此事。

en: If you do this, God will direct you, you will be able to endure, and all this people also will go to their place in peace."
zh: 你若是這樣行， 神也這樣吩咐你，你就能站立得住，所有的人民也都能平平安安地歸回自己的地方。”

en: So Moses listened to the voice of his father-in-law and did all that he had said.
zh: 於是，摩西聽從他岳父的話，照著他所說的行了。

en: Moses chose able men out of all Israel and made them heads over the people, chiefs of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens.
zh: 摩西從全體以色列人中選出了有才能的人，立他們作人民的首領，作千夫長、百夫長、五十夫長、十夫長。

en: And they judged the people at all times. Any hard case they brought to Moses, but any small matter they decided themselves.
zh: 他們隨時審判人民的案件；有困難的事件，他們就帶到摩西那裡；所有小事件，由他們自己審判。

en: Then Moses let his father-in-law depart, and he went away to his own country.
zh: 後來，摩西給他的岳父送行，他就回自己的地方去了。
